description: Residents in Katherine have woken up to assess the damage after the town experienced its worst flooding in 28 years. title: Government considering military assistance request from Gulf States | Insiders | ABC NEWS
description: The federal government is considering a request for military assistance from Persian Gulf States targeted by Iranian attacks as the war in the Middle East enters its second week.   All six Gulf States — Oman, the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar — have been impacted by Iranian strikes that have targeted civilian infrastructure, including airports, since the beginning of the war.    Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong told Insiders the government had received requests from some of those countries for protection against drone and missile attacks.   She would not provide details on the type of military assistance being considered, but said the government would be transparent if it accepted the request.   The minister maintained the government's position that it would not participate in offensive action against Iran. title: Labor on track to win Nightcliff by-election, taking NT's single Greens seat | ABC NEWS
description: Labor is on track to win the Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' first and only seat held in the Northern Territory parliament. title: Katherine experiences worst flooding in 28 years as remote NT communities evacuated | ABC NEWS
description: Katherine has experienced its worst flooding in 28 years, with several Territorians rescued by helicopter this weekend after they were stranded in a nearby remote community. Authorities received 120 calls for help across the Top End over the 24 hours to Sunday afternoon, with roughly 1,000 flood evacuees currently being housed in temporary shelters. The Katherine River peaked at 19.2 metres around 10:45pm on Saturday, inundating homes and businesses, but has been falling since. Hundreds of millimetres of rain have been dumped over catchments in recent days, sending a surge of water down several rivers. It caused the town of Daly River, also known as Nauiyu, to reach and exceed the major flood level of 14 metres on Saturday. Daly River residents are expected to endure another weeks-long stay at the Foskey Pavilion emergency evacuation centre in Darwin's showgrounds. Further south-west of Nauiyu, about 200 residents were also evacuated by air from the remote community of Palumpa — also known as Nganmarriyanga — as floodwaters inundated a number of homes. By James Elton and Joseph Hathaway-Wilson for ABC News Northern Territory. title: Cory Bernardi stands by bestiality claim ahead of SA election | ABC NEWS
description: Cory Bernardi, who is One Nation's lead candidate in South Australia, says he stands by previous comments he made linking gay marriage to bestiality and polygamy.  He told ABC Stateline he was "not fussed" if people found his comments offensive. He also outlined the party's policies on education and health.  He says One Nation is challenging what he branded the "uni-party", comprised of Labor and the Liberals, at the upcoming state election.
